Ntc Of Special Mtn To Strike Complaint For Malicious Prosecution
Off Calendar Feb-05-2008 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: DEFENDANT ROBERT HUBERMAN'S SPECIAL MOTION TO STRIKE COMPLAINT FOR MALICIOUS PROSECUTION.
APPEARANCES: STEVEN BERNIKER, ESQUIRE (707.542.5132) APPEARING FOR PLAINTIFF. JOHN P. BABA, ESQUIRE (WIEGEL & FRIED, 415.552.8230) APPEARING FOR DEFENDANT.
RULING: ARGUED AND THE COURT ADOPTS ITS TENTATIVE RULING: GRANTED. THE CENTRAL ISSUE IS PROBABLE CAUSE BY WELCH TO SEEK DAMAGES UNDER CIVIL CODE SECTION 3346. THE COURT FINDS THAT A REASONABLE ATTORNEY WOULD FIND THAT SECTION 3346 WOULD SUPPORT A CLAIM FOR DAMAGES TO THE VINES AND THE TREE LIMB ON THE WELCH PROPERTY. THIS CONCLUSION IS SUPPORTED BY THE DECSION IN HENINGER V DUNN (1980) 101 CAL APP 3D 858, 866. FOR THAT REASON, STEFFEN CANNOT ESTABLISH PROBABLE CAUSE. STEFFEN'S OBJECTIONS TO THE WELCH AND WIEGEL DECLARATIONS ARE OVERRULED. MR. BABA TO SUBMIT A FORM OF ORDER. (302/PJM/AY) JUDGE: PATRICK J. MAHONEY, REPORTER: MARIA TORREANO, CSR 8600
